__description: The capture module provides camera live preview, snapshot, and video recording capabilities
__tinydesc: Camera capture, live preview, snapshot and video recording

devices.__tinydesc: Get the list of available video and audio capture devices
devices.__description:
Returns a table listing all available capture devices on the system. The table contains two fields:
  • video: an array of string values, each being the name of an available video capture device (e.g. a webcam)
  • audio: an array of string values, each being the name of an available audio capture device (e.g. a microphone)
The device names returned here can be passed directly to the Camera constructor to select a specific device.
devices.__example:
--! luart-extensions

import capture

print("Available video devices:")
for name in each(capture.devices.video) do
    print("\t${name}")
end

print("Available audio devices:")
for name in each(capture.devices.audio) do
    print("\t${name}")
end

Camera.__description: The Camera object manages a video capture device
Camera.__tinydesc: Object for using video capture devices

constructor.__description:
The Camera constructor initializes a new camera capture session using the specified devices.
The constructor blocks until the first camera frame has been received, ensuring the Camera is ready to use immediately after construction.

Widget vs Object mode

  • Camera behaves differently depending on whether the ui module has been loaded before capture.
  • When ui is loaded first, Camera is a widget (available in the ui module), that renders a live preview inside a child window. It requires a parent widget and accepts position and size arguments.
  • When ui is not loaded, Camera is a plain object used for headless capture only (snapshot and recording). No preview window is created and no positional arguments are accepted.

Notes

  • If no devices table is provided, the system default video and audio devices are used.
  • Raises an error if no video or audio device is found on the system.
constructor.__returns: Returns a new Camera instance, or raises an error if initialization fails.

__params.__description:
An optional table specifying which capture devices to use. It may contain:
  • video: the name of the video capture device as a string (from capture.devices.video)
  • audio: the name of the audio capture device as a string (from capture.devices.audio)
If omitted, the system default devices are used.
The parent widget. Required when using Camera as a widget (i.e. when the ui module is loaded first).
Horizontal position of the Camera widget in pixels, relative to its parent. Defaults to 0. Only used in widget mode.
Vertical position of the Camera widget in pixels, relative to its parent. Defaults to 0. Only used in widget mode.
Width of the Camera widget in pixels. Defaults to 320. Only used in widget mode.
Height of the Camera widget in pixels. Defaults to 240. Only used in widget mode.

constructor.__example:
--! luart-extensions

import ui, capture

-- Camera as a widget using the default devices
local win = ui.Window("Camera Preview", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)
cam.align = "all"
cam.aspect = "16x9"

await win:showasync()

snapshot.__tinydesc: Save a snapshot of the current camera frame to a file
snapshot.__description:
Captures the most recently received camera frame and saves it to the specified file.
The output format is determined by the file extension. Supported formats are .png, .jpg / .jpeg, and .bmp.

Notes

  • Returns false if no frame has been received yet or if the file cannot be written.
  • The Camera constructor already waits for the first frame, so a snapshot taken immediately after construction will succeed.
snapshot.__returns: Returns true if the snapshot was saved successfully, or false if an error occurred.

__params.__description: The destination file path as a string or a File. The file extension determines the image format (.png, .jpg, .bmp).

snapshot.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import capture

local cam = capture.Camera()

if cam:snapshot("photo.png") then
    print("Snapshot saved to photo.png")
else
    print("Failed to save snapshot")
end

aspect.__tinydesc: Get or set the preview aspect ratio mode
aspect.__description:
Controls how the live camera preview is scaled inside the Camera widget. Accepted values are:
  • "stretch": the camera image is stretched to fill the entire widget area, ignoring the original aspect ratio
  • "4x3": the image is letterboxed horizontally to preserve a 4:3 aspect ratio
  • "16x9": the image is letterboxed vertically to preserve a 16:9 aspect ratio
aspect.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

-- Switch to 16:9 letterbox mode
cam.aspect = "16x9"
print("Current aspect mode: "..cam.aspect)

await win:showasync()

recording.__tinydesc: Check whether the camera is currently recording
recording.__description:
Returns true if a recording session is currently active, or false otherwise.
Use the record property to start and stop recording.
recording.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import capture

local cam = capture.Camera()

print("Recording: "..tostring(cam.recording))

cam.record.start { file = "output.mp4" }
print("Recording: "..tostring(cam.recording))

sleep(5000)

cam.record.stop()
print("Recording: "..tostring(cam.recording))

record.__description:
Returns a table containing two functions to control video recording:
  • record.start(options): starts recording to a file. The options argument is a table with the following fields:
    • file (required): the destination file path as a string. The extension determines the container format: .mp4, .wmv, or .avi
    • format (optional): the video encoding quality as a string, one of "Auto", "VGA", "HD720p" (default), "HD1080p", "UHD2k", "UHD4k"
    • video (optional): the video bitrate preset as a string, one of "low" (1 Mbps), "medium" (2 Mbps), "high" (5 Mbps, default), "ultra" (10 Mbps)
    • audio (optional): the audio bitrate preset as a string, one of "low" (64 kbps), "medium" (128 kbps, default), "high" (192 kbps), "ultra" (256 kbps)
  • record.stop(): stops the current recording session. Has no effect if no recording is active.

Notes

  • Calling record.start() while already recording will automatically stop the previous session before starting a new one.
  • Audio is always recorded in stereo at 44100 Hz sample rate.
  • Raises an error if the file field is missing or the file extension is not supported.
record.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import capture

local cam = capture.Camera()

-- Start recording an MP4 at 1080p with high video bitrate
cam.record.start {
    file   = "output.mp4",
    format = "HD1080p",
    video  = "high",
    audio  = "medium"
}

print("Recording 5sec...")
sleep(5000)

cam.record.stop()
print("Recording stopped")

border.__tinydesc: Get or set the Camera widget border visibility (widget only)
border.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Controls whether the Camera widget displays a border.
Returns true if the widget border is visible, false otherwise. Set to true to show the border or false to hide it.
border.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

-- Hide the widget border
cam.border = false

await win:showasync()

x.__tinydesc: Get or set the Camera widget horizontal position (widget only)
x.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget horizontal position. The horizontal position starts from 0 (left side of the parent widget) and increases to the right.
x.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.x sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 300, 240)

-- Move Camera to the right
cam.x = 200

await win:showasync()

y.__tinydesc: Get or set the Camera widget vertical position (widget only)
y.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget vertical position. The vertical position starts from 0 (top side of the parent widget) and increases downward.
y.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.y sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 300, 240)

-- Move Camera down
cam.y = 100

await win:showasync()

width.__tinydesc: Get or set the Camera widget width (widget only)
width.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget width. The width starts from 0 (left side of the Camera) and increases to the right.
width.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.width sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 300, 240)

print("Camera width: "..cam.width)

await win:showasync()

height.__tinydesc: Get or set the Camera widget height (widget only)
height.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget height. The height starts from 0 (top side of the Camera) and increases downward.
height.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.height sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 300, 240)

print("Camera height: "..cam.height)

await win:showasync()

parent.__tinydesc: Get the Camera widget's parent widget (widget only)
parent.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get the parent widget that owns the Camera widget.
parent.__results: Window | Panel | Groupbox
parent.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.parent sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

print("Camera parent is a "..type(cam.parent))

await win:showasync()

visible.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Camera widget appears on screen (widget only)
visible.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget visibility. A true value means the Camera is shown; false means it is hidden.
This property is also affected by the Camera:show() and Camera:hide() methods.
visible.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.visible sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

-- Toggle Camera visibility
cam.visible = false
sleep(2000)
cam.visible = true

await win:showasync()

enabled.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Camera widget responds to events (widget only)
enabled.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget's ability to respond to mouse and other events. When set to false, the Camera is disabled and its events will no longer be fired.
enabled.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.enabled sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

win:showasync()

-- Disable Camera
cam.enabled = false

sleep(3000)

-- Enable Camera
cam.enabled = true

waitall()

align.__tinydesc: Align the Camera widget relative to its parent (widget only)
align.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget alignment relative to its parent. The alignment persists even if the parent is resized.
Accepted values are:
  • "all": covers the entire parent client area
  • "bottom": aligned along the bottom border, preserving height
  • "top": aligned along the top border, preserving height
  • "right": aligned along the right border, preserving width
  • "left": aligned along the left border, preserving width
align.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.align sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 0, 0, 640, 460)

-- Fill the entire parent window
cam.align = "all"

await win:showasync()

cursor.__tinydesc: Get or set the mouse cursor when hovering the Camera widget (widget only)
cursor.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the mouse cursor image as it hovers over the Camera widget. Accepted values are: "arrow", "cross", "working", "hand", "help", "ibeam", "forbidden", "cardinal", "horizontal", "vertical", "leftdiagonal", "rightdiagonal", "up", "wait", "none" or nil.
cursor.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.cursor sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

-- Show a crosshair cursor over the Camera preview
cam.cursor = "cross"

await win:showasync()

tooltip.__tinydesc: Get or set the Camera widget tooltip text (widget only)
tooltip.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the tooltip text that appears when the user moves the mouse over the Camera widget, as a string.
Set to an empty string to remove the tooltip.
tooltip.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.tooltip sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

cam.tooltip = "Live camera preview"

await win:showasync()

allowdrop.__tinydesc: Controls whether the Camera widget is a drag and drop target (widget only)
allowdrop.__description:
widget only

This property is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Get or set the Camera widget's ability to be used as the target of a drag-and-drop operation. When set to true, the Camera:onDrop() event will be fired when content is dropped on the widget.
allowdrop.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera.allowdrop sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

cam.allowdrop = true

function cam:onDrop(kind, content)
    ui.info("Dropped: "..kind)
end

await win:showasync()

show.__tinydesc: Show and activate the Camera widget (widget only)
show.__description:
widget only

This method is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Show and activate the Camera widget. Events can be fired once the widget is shown.
show.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:show() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

cam:hide()
sleep(2000)
cam:show()

await win:showasync()

hide.__tinydesc: Hide and deactivate the Camera widget (widget only)
hide.__description:
widget only

This method is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Hide and deactivate the Camera widget. Events can no longer be fired while the widget is hidden.
hide.__example:
--! luart-extensions

import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:hide() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

win:showasync()

sleep(3000)
cam:hide()

waitall()

center.__tinydesc: Center the Camera widget on its parent (widget only)
center.__description:
widget only

This method is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Centers the Camera widget on its parent widget.
center.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:center() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 0, 0, 320, 240)

cam:center()

await win:showasync()

tofront.__tinydesc: Bring the Camera widget in front of other widgets (widget only)
tofront.__description:
widget only

This method is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Brings the Camera widget in front of all other widgets, or in front of the specified widget.

__params.__description: An optional widget above which the Camera will be placed.

tofront.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:toback() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 20, 620, 460)
local lbl = ui.Label(win, capture.devices.video[1] or error("No Webcam detected"))

cam:tofront()

await win:showasync()

toback.__tinydesc: Put the Camera widget behind other widgets (widget only)
toback.__description:
widget only

This method is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget. Accessing it in object mode will raise an error.
Puts the Camera widget behind all other widgets, or behind the specified widget.

__params.__description: An optional widget below which the Camera will be placed.

toback.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:toback() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 34, 620, 460)
local lbl = ui.Label(win, capture.devices.video[1] or "No Webcam detected")

win:showasync()

lbl.fontsize = 18
lbl.y = cam.y
cam:toback()

waitall()

onCreate.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Camera widget has just been created (widget only)
onCreate.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the Camera widget has just been created, right after the constructor returns. It is a good place to initialize widget properties.
onCreate.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onCreate() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onCreate()
    self.tooltip = "Camera ready"
    self.cursor = "cross"
end

await win:showasync()

onShow.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Camera widget is shown (widget only)
onShow.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the Camera widget is shown, either via Camera:show() or by setting the Camera.visible property to true.
onShow.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onShow() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onShow()
    print("Camera widget is now visible")
end

await win:showasync()

onHide.__tinydesc: Event fired when the Camera widget is hidden (widget only)
onHide.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the Camera widget is hidden, either via Camera:hide() or by setting the Camera.visible property to false.
onHide.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onHide() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onHide()
    print("Camera widget is now hidden")
end

await win:showasync()

onClick.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user left-clicks on the Camera widget (widget only)
onClick.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the user left-clicks on the Camera widget.
onClick.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onClick() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onClick()
    -- Take a snapshot on click
    cam:snapshot("click_snapshot.png")
    print("Snapshot taken!")
end

await win:showasync()

onContext.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user right-clicks on the Camera widget (widget only)
onContext.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the user right-clicks on the Camera widget.
onContext.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onContext() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onContext()
    print("Right-clicked on Camera")
end

await win:showasync()

onHover.__tinydesc: Event fired when the mouse moves over the Camera widget (widget only)
onHover.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the user moves the mouse pointer over the Camera widget.

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Camera widget area (zero means the left border).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Camera widget area (zero means the top border).

A table indicating which mouse buttons or special keys are pressed:

  • "left": true if the left mouse button is pressed
  • "right": true if the right mouse button is pressed
  • "middle": true if the middle mouse button is pressed
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT key is pressed

onHover.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onHover() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onHover(x, y, buttons)
    win:status("Mouse at "..x..", "..y)
end

await win:showasync()

onLeave.__tinydesc: Event fired when the mouse cursor leaves the Camera widget (widget only)
onLeave.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the mouse cursor leaves the Camera widget area.
onLeave.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onLeave() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onLeave()
    print("Mouse left the Camera preview area")
end

await win:showasync()

onMouseDown.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user presses a mouse button over the Camera widget (widget only)
onMouseDown.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the user presses a mouse button while over the Camera widget.

__params.__description: A string indicating which button was pressed: "left", "right", or "middle".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Camera widget area (zero means the left border).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Camera widget area (zero means the top border).

onMouseDown.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onMouseDown() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onMouseDown(button, x, y)
    win:status(button.." button pressed at "..x..", "..y)
end

await win:showasync()

onMouseUp.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user releases a mouse button over the Camera widget (widget only)
onMouseUp.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the user releases a mouse button while over the Camera widget.

__params.__description: A string indicating which button was released: "left", "right", or "middle".
The horizontal position of the mouse in the Camera widget area (zero means the left border).
The vertical position of the mouse in the Camera widget area (zero means the top border).

onMouseUp.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onMouseUp() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

function cam:onMouseUp(button, x, y)
    win:status(button.." button released at "..x..", "..y)
end

await win:showasync()

onDrop.__tinydesc: Event fired when content is dropped on the Camera widget (widget only)
onDrop.__description:
widget only

This event is only available when the ui module is loaded before capture and Camera is used as a widget.
This event is fired when the user drops content onto the Camera widget. The Camera.allowdrop property must be set to true for this event to fire.

__params.__description: A string representing the kind of content dropped: "text", "files", or "unknown".

The dropped content. Type depends on kind:

kindcontent
"text"string
"files"table (list of File and Directory)
"unknown"nil

onDrop.__example:
--! luart-extensions
import ui, capture

local win = ui.Window("Camera:onDrop() sample", "fixed", 640, 520)
local cam = ui.Camera(win, 10, 10, 620, 460)

cam.allowdrop = true

function cam:onDrop(kind, content)
    ui.info("Dropped "..kind.." on Camera")
end

await win:showasync()
